name: Horizon
description_short: Horizon Credit Union is a member-owned financial cooperative with 13,000+ members offering auto loans, mortgages, credit cards, and digital banking services.
description_long:
Horizon Credit Union (HCU) has been operating for over 60 years as a member-owned, non-profit financial institution guided by the credit union philosophy of 'people helping people.' Based on their website (myhorizoncu.com), they serve more than 13,000 members across their service area. As a credit union, HCU does not issue stock to outside investors; instead, earnings are returned directly to members through competitive rates, lower fees, and enhanced services. This fundamental business model distinguishes them from traditional banks that prioritize shareholder returns.

Horizon Credit Union offers a comprehensive suite of consumer financial products including auto loans (as low as 4.84% APR), RV loans (as low as 7.19% APR), credit cards (as low as 9.9% APR), home mortgages for first-time and experienced homeowners, and general everyday financial services. Members also have access to surcharge-free ATM access through the Co-op network of nearly 30,000 ATMs nationwide, mobile banking, insurance and planning services, and personal loan products branded as 'Go LOANS' that can be applied for and signed online.

What distinguishes Horizon is their emphasis on community-based, personalized financial partnerships. They explicitly position themselves as serving their local neighborhood with member relationship focus and staff they describe as 'personal financial partners dedicated to your success.' Their digital investment in online and mobile banking reflects an effort to balance traditional credit union service with modern convenience. The company actively recruits employees and frames employment as a mission-driven opportunity where workers are 'respected and valued.'

For consumers evaluating Horizon, the main consideration is membership eligibility—credit unions typically have specific membership criteria based on geography, employment, or affiliation. Their rates are competitive (4.84% auto loans, 9.9% credit cards), but the website lacks details on membership requirements, specific eligibility criteria, fee schedules, or rates for savings accounts and CDs. The routing number (324377011) and phone number (801.451.5064) suggest a Utah-based institution with likely regional service limitations. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Home mortgages for first-time and existing homeowners
Go LOANS personal loans with online application and signing
Surcharge-free ATM access through Co-op network of 30,000 ATMs
Mobile banking and digital account management
Everyday finance products and member savings accounts
Checking and savings accounts (implied through banking services)
pros: Member-owned non-profit structure returns earnings to members as competitive rates and lower fees instead of shareholder profits
Access to nearly 30,000 surcharge-free ATMs through Co-op network, providing widespread cash access
Auto loans starting at 4.84% APR and RV loans at 7.19% APR are competitively priced
Online loan application and digital signing available for Go LOANS, eliminating in-person visit requirements
Over 60 years of operating history with 13,000+ established members providing stability
Comprehensive product suite including mortgages, auto loans, credit cards, insurance, and mobile banking
Staff positioned as 'personal financial partners' with emphasis on individualized member service
cons: Website does not disclose membership eligibility requirements or geographic service limitations
No fee schedule or savings account/CD rates displayed on website for comparison purposes
Credit card rates start at 9.9% which is higher than many online credit card issuers
Limited transparency about mortgage product details (loan types, terms, down payment requirements)
As a credit union, likely requires membership verification before account opening, creating friction versus direct consumer access at banks
best_for: Utah-based residents or those in the credit union's service area seeking member-owned financial institution benefits
Auto and RV buyers prioritizing competitive loan rates with personalized local service
Consumers who value credit union philosophy of 'people helping people' over traditional bank structure
Individuals seeking convenient ATM access through national Co-op network while maintaining local relationship banking
diagnosis: Horizon Credit Union is best suited for eligible members in their service area seeking competitive consumer lending rates combined with community-focused, personalized financial service. The primary caveat is that credit union membership requires eligibility verification (likely geographic or affiliation-based), and the website lacks clarity on membership requirements, fee structures, and savings product rates needed for complete financial evaluation.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
